title: Ordering, Single Active Consumer, and Partitioning · Execution Trace: Ordering Scope Contract
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must scope order, partition by stable key, detect versions and gaps, and measure failover under bounded concurrency; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ordering scope contract increment in a keyed order and failover state machine in Go. Learners progress thr…
objectives: State ordering scope across producer channel, exchange route, one queue, delivery, completion, redelivery, priority, and multiple consumers; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Gate RabbitMQ 4.3 behavior on deployed broker version, queue type, policy, feature state, amqp091-go behavior, migration state, and tested fallback.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Ordering Scope Contract · Theory Ordering Scope Contract
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 2465 during canary Go consumer decoding old and new envelopes. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce keyed sequence progression and single-active failover limits; prove cleanup under a single deadline and cancellation cause; then lower the byte and goroutine budget and preserve rejection evidence. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
